doc.id: Potkay and Feng - 2023 - Do stomata optimize turgor-driven growth A new framework for integrating stomata response with whol_appendix.pdf
doc.title: Do stomata optimize turgor-driven growth? A new framework for integrating stomata response with whole-tree carbon and hydraulics (Supporting Information)

model_card.name: S6 Turgor-limited potential growth
task: Compute sink-limited potential growth rate G0 driven by turgor
Compute sensitivity dG0/dE needed for stomatal growth optimization

assumptions.text: Whole-stem growth can be represented by scaling the Lockhart (1965) turgor-driven expansion equation to the whole-stem scale (allometric architecture assumptions).

evidence.snippet: The sink-limited potential growth rate has been formulated by Potkay et al. (2021b) who scaled the Lockhart (1965) equation for turgor-driven cell expansion to the whole-stem scale

assumptions.text: Inner bark (phloem) water potential is approximated by xylem water potential via xylem–phloem coupling (water-potential equilibrium).

evidence.snippet: xylem-phloem coupling enables water-potential equilibrium ... thus we may approximate the inner bark potential as the xylem potential.

assumptions.text: Osmotic potential (π and π0) is treated as axially uniform within the stem as a first-order approximation.

evidence.snippet: we approximate π (and π0 in Eq. S6.2) as axially uniform within the stem as a first-order approximation

assumptions.text: Non-NSC-limited osmotic potential π0 is estimated empirically from Scots pine regressions linking ψS to phloem sap molality mp and then π0 via a van ’t Hoff relation (osmolytes behave like sucrose).
To remove the discontinuity from max(·,0) in Eq. S6.1, integration is performed only over the axial domain where P0(z̃) ≥ Γ using a threshold location z̃+.

evidence.snippet: change the bounds of integration ... integrate only over the z̃ domain for which P0(z̃) ≥ Γ ... (S6.5)

equations.explanation: Definition of sink-limited potential growth rate as whole-stem integral of excess turgor above threshold.
Potential (NSC-unlimited) turgor pressure is water potential minus osmotic potential (NSC-unlimited).
Empirical regression for phloem sap molality vs stem-apex water potential (Scots pine).
Nonlinear van 't Hoff-type formula for osmotic potential (osmolytes behave like sucrose).
Reformulation integrating only where P0 ≥ Γ to avoid max(·,0) discontinuity.
Threshold axial location where P0 equals Γ, clipped to [0,1].
Analytical expression for G0 after combining Eq. S6.2&5 with stem hydraulics (Eq. S5.6 referenced).

evidence.snippet: an analytical expression for the sink-limited potential growth rate is given by ... (S6.7)

equations.explanation: Defines Ω in terms of the dilogarithm (polylogarithm of order 2).
Integral definition of the dilogarithm used in Eq. S6.8.
Auxiliary function Λ(z̃) used in Ω(z̃+) and in dG0/dE.

evidence.snippet: Λ(\tilde{z}) = exp[\tilde{z} (\alphaS/kS)(χS + aL E)] {1 + (χS/(aL E) + 1) exp[αS(ψS − βS)]}. (S6.10)

equations.latex: \frac{\partial G_0}{\partial E}=\tilde{\phi}\,\frac{C_W}{u_S}\left\{ -\frac{\partial\tilde{z}^{+}}{\partial E}\left\u005b\beta_S-\pi_0-\Gamma-\frac{1}{\alpha_S}\ln\left(\frac{\chi_S}{a_L E}+1\right)\right\u005d+(1-\tilde{z}^{+})\left\u005b\frac{\chi_S}{\alpha_S E(\chi_S+a_L E)}-\frac{\partial\pi_0}{\partial\psi_S}\frac{\partial\psi_S}{\partial E}\right\u005d-\frac{a_L k_S\Omega}{\alpha_S^2(\chi_S+a_L E)^2}+\frac{k_S\ln\u005b\Lambda(1)-1\u005d}{\alpha_S^2(\chi_S+a_L E)\Lambda(1)}\frac{\partial\Lambda(1)}{\partial E}-\frac{k_S\ln\u005b\Lambda(\tilde{z}^{+})-1\u005d}{\alpha_S^2(\chi_S+a_L E)\Lambda(\tilde{z}^{+})}\frac{\partial\Lambda(\tilde{z}^{+})}{\partial E}\right\}
equations.explanation: Sensitivity of sink-limited potential growth with respect to transpiration rate E.
Derivative of π0 with respect to stem-apex water potential (via Eqs S6.3–S6.4).

equations.latex: \frac{\partial\tilde{z}^{+}}{\partial E}=\begin{cases}0, & \tilde{z}^{+}=0 \\ \frac{1}{\chi_S+a_L E}\left\{k_S\left(\frac{\partial\pi_0}{\partial\psi_S}-1\right)\frac{\partial\psi_S}{\partial E}-a_L\tilde{z}^{+}+\frac{k_S}{\alpha_S}\left\u005b\frac{a_L e^{-\alpha_S\beta_S}+a_L\left(1-\alpha_S E\frac{\partial\pi_0}{\partial\psi_S}\frac{\partial\psi_S}{\partial E}\right)e^{-\alpha_S\Gamma-\alpha_S\pi_0}}{(\chi_S+a_L E)e^{-\alpha_S\beta_S}+a_L E e^{-\alpha_S\Gamma-\alpha_S\pi_0}}-\frac{a_L e^{-\alpha_S\beta_S}+a_L\left(1-\alpha_S E\frac{\partial\psi_S}{\partial E}\right)e^{-\alpha_S\psi_S}}{(\chi_S+a_L E)e^{-\alpha_S\beta_S}+a_L E e^{-\alpha_S\psi_S}}\right\u005b\right\}\right\}, & 0<\tilde{z}^{+}<1 \\ 0, & \tilde{z}^{+}=1\end{cases}
equations.explanation: Piecewise derivative of the threshold location z̃+ with respect to E (by differentiating Eq. S6.6).

evidence.snippet: ∂\tilde{z}^+/∂E is defined by differentiating Eq. S6.6 ... (S6.13)

equations.explanation: Derivative of Λ(1) with respect to E from differentiating Eq. S6.10.

equations.latex: \frac{\partial\Lambda(\tilde{z}^{+})}{\partial E}=\frac{\exp\left\u005b\tilde{z}^{+}\frac{\alpha_S}{k_S}(\chi_S+a_L E)\u005d}{a_L E}\left\{\left\u005b(\chi_S+a_L E)\alpha_S\frac{\partial\psi_S}{\partial E}-\frac{\chi_S}{E}\right\u005d\exp\big(\alpha_S(\psi_S-\beta_S)\big)+\frac{\alpha_S}{k_S}\left\u005ba_L E+(\chi_S+a_L E)\exp\big(\alpha_S(\psi_S-\beta_S)\big)\right\u005d\left\u005b(\chi_S+a_L E)\frac{\partial\tilde{z}^{+}}{\partial E}+a_L\tilde{z}^{+}\right\u005d\right\}
equations.explanation: Derivative of Λ(z̃+) with respect to E from differentiating Eq. S6.10.

variables.name: sink-limited potential growth rate
variables.definition: Potential whole-stem growth rate limited by turgor (not by NSC substrate), integrated along the stem.

evidence.snippet: sink-limited potential growth rate, G0 [mol C·s-1]

variables.name: leaf-area specific transpiration rate
variables.definition: Leaf-area specific transpiration rate used as the driver of water potential gradients and thus turgor.

evidence.snippet: G0 may be written as a function defined solely by the leaf-area specific transpiration rate, E [mol H2O·m-2·s-1] ...

variables.name: leaf area
variables.definition: Leaf area used in scaling transpiration and hydraulic terms.

variables.name: air temperature
variables.definition: Air temperature used for temperature dependence of extensibility and osmotic potential.

variables.name: woody stem biomass in carbon equivalents
variables.definition: Woody stem biomass expressed in carbon equivalents.

evidence.snippet: CW is the woody stem biomass in C equivalents [mol C]

variables.name: fraction of growth allocated to woody stem growth
variables.definition: Allocation fraction mapping whole-tree growth to woody stem growth.

evidence.snippet: uS is the fraction of growth allocated to woody stem growth

variables.name: axial coordinate from stem apex
variables.definition: Normalized axial distance from the stem apex; same coordinate as in Section S5.

evidence.snippet: z̃ is the axial distance from the stem-apex (i.e., the same coordinate used in Section S5)

variables.name: NSC-unlimited potential turgor pressure
variables.definition: Potential turgor pressure along the stem if NSCs were not limiting.

evidence.snippet: P0 is axially heterogeneous potential turgor pressure if NSCs were not limiting [MPa]

variables.name: threshold pressure for tissue expansion
variables.definition: Turgor threshold that must be exceeded before tissue expansion occurs.

evidence.snippet: Γ is the threshold pressure that turgor must exceed before tissue expansion occurs [MPa]

variables.name: xylem water potential along stem
variables.definition: Water potential along the stem determined by xylem hydraulics (referencing Eq. S5.5).

evidence.snippet: ψ is defined by xylem hydraulics (Eq. S5.5)

variables.name: stem-apex water potential
variables.definition: Stem apex water potential used to estimate mp and π0.

variables.name: NSC-unlimited osmotic potential
variables.definition: Osmotic potential if NSCs were not limiting (more negative than or equal to true π with NSC limitations).

evidence.snippet: π0 is the osmotic potential if NSCs were not limiting [MPa]

variables.name: phloem sap molality
variables.definition: Phloem sap molality predicted from ψS using an empirical regression.

variables.name: effective whole-tree extensibility
variables.definition: Effective extensibility parameter capturing temperature sensitivity of growth; proportional to true extensibility ϕ by an allometric factor.

variables.name: threshold axial location where P0 equals Γ
variables.definition: Location (in normalized axial coordinate) where P0(z̃)=Γ, clipped to [0,1].

evidence.snippet: z̃+ is the value of z̃ at which P0 = Γ if located within the bounds of z̃ (0 ≤ z̃+ ≤ 1).

variables.name: unitless shorthand for polylogarithm expression
variables.definition: Auxiliary function defined in Eq. S6.8 using Li2 and Λ.

evidence.snippet: where Ω is unitless shorthand for (S6.8)

variables.name: auxiliary function Lambda
variables.definition: Auxiliary unitless function defined in Eq. S6.10.

evidence.snippet: Λ is a unitless function of z̃ defined as (S6.10)

variables.name: soil water potential
variables.definition: Soil water potential input; used as an argument for G0 via hydraulics (referenced in text).

variables.name: stem hydraulic parameter kS
variables.definition: Stem hydraulic parameter appearing in z̃+ and G0 expressions; defined in Section S5 (referenced).

variables.name: stem vulnerability curve shape parameter αS
variables.definition: Shape parameter appearing in analytical stem water potential and growth expressions; defined in Section S5 (referenced).

variables.name: stem water potential at 50% conductance loss βS
variables.definition: Parameter appearing in analytical expressions; defined in Section S5 (referenced).

variables.name: gravitational/hydrostatic term χS
variables.definition: Hydraulic term appearing in stem water potential solutions; defined in Section S5 (referenced).

parameters.name: c_{m,1}
parameters.estimation: Empirical coefficient for Scots pine regression (Paljakka et al., 2017).

parameters.name: c_{m,2}
parameters.estimation: Empirical coefficient for Scots pine regression (Paljakka et al., 2017).

parameters.name: c_{\Pi,1}
parameters.estimation: Empirical coefficient in osmotic potential formula (Michel, 1972; Thompson & Holbrook, 2003a).

parameters.name: c_{\Pi,2}
parameters.estimation: Empirical coefficient in osmotic potential formula (Michel, 1972; Thompson & Holbrook, 2003a).

parameters.name: \varpi
parameters.estimation: Unit conversion factor from Pa to MPa.

parameters.name: a (allometry exponent)
parameters.estimation: Classical exponent relating tree height and DBH in allometric scaling.

evidence.snippet: For classical values of a = 3/2 ... and b = 2/3 ... ϕ̃ = 4ϕ.

parameters.name: b (phloem thickness exponent)
parameters.estimation: Classical exponent relating phloem thickness and stem diameter in allometric scaling.

evidence.snippet: For classical values of a = 3/2 ... and b = 2/3 ... ϕ̃ = 4ϕ.

inputs: Hydraulic parameters from Section S5 (k_S, \alpha_S, \beta_S, \chi_S, and \psi(\tilde{z}), \psi_S, \partial\psi_S/\partial E)
steps: Compute stem water potential profile ψ( z̃ ) and stem-apex potential ψS using xylem hydraulics (referenced Eq. S5.5 and Eq. S5.6).
Compute phloem sap molality mp from ψS using Eq. S6.3.
Compute NSC-unlimited osmotic potential π0 from mp and Ta using Eq. S6.4.
Compute threshold location z̃+ where P0(z̃)=Γ using Eq. S6.6 (clipped to [0,1]).
Compute analytical sink-limited potential growth G0 using Eq. S6.7 with Ω(z̃+) (Eqs. S6.8–S6.10).
Compute ∂π0/∂ψS (Eq. S6.12), ∂z̃+/∂E (Eq. S6.13), ∂Λ(1)/∂E (Eq. S6.14), ∂Λ(z̃+)/∂E (Eq. S6.15), and assemble ∂G0/∂E using Eq. S6.11.

evidence.snippet: we required solutions for how the sink-limited growth rate changes ... ∂G0/∂E is solved by differentiating Eq. S6.7 with respect to E

limitations.text: π0 is estimated using an empirical Scots pine regression (R2=0.44) and may not generalize across species or conditions without recalibration.

evidence.snippet: Paljakka et al., 2017; R2 = 0.44; Eqn S6.3&4

limitations.text: Assumes axially uniform π (and π0) within the stem as a first-order simplification; real stems may show gradients.

evidence.snippet: we approximate π (and π0 in Eq. S6.2) as axially uniform within the stem as a first-order approximation for simplicity

limitations.text: Approximates inner bark potential by xylem potential; validity relies on xylem–phloem coupling assumptions.

evidence.snippet: xylem-phloem coupling enables water-potential equilibrium ... approximate the inner bark potential as the xylem potential

implementation.model_scope: Section S6 provides an analytical formulation of sink-limited potential growth G0 and its derivative with respect to transpiration E for use in growth-optimizing stomatal conductance (Section S2).

evidence.snippet: G0 may be written as a function ... (ψsoil, E, Ta)

unresolved_assets.label: Eq. S5.5 / Eq. S5.6 (ψ(z̃), ψS)
unresolved_assets.issue: This section requires ψ(z̃) and ψS from xylem hydraulics in Section S5 to evaluate P0(z̃) and z̃+.

evidence.snippet: ψ is defined by xylem hydraulics (Eq. S5.5)
z̃+ can be solved ... by setting ψ(z̃+) = Γ + π0 ... (S6.6)

unresolved_assets.label: ∂ψS/∂E (referenced from Section S5)
unresolved_assets.issue: dG0/dE requires ∂ψS/∂E, which is referenced as already defined in Section S5 but not re-derived here.

evidence.snippet: where ∂ψS/∂E was already defined by Eq. S5.10

open_questions.question: What are the numerical evaluation details/constraints for Li2(·) and logarithms in Ω(z̃+) and Eq. S6.11 to ensure stable computation across parameter ranges?
open_questions.why_needed: Implementation of Eq. S6.7–S6.11 depends on special functions and log terms that may encounter domain issues (e.g., Λ(·) close to 1).
open_questions.likely_location: Implementation notes, code repository, or numerical methods section (not shown in S6).

evidence.snippet: terms ln[Λ(1)−1] and ln[Λ(z̃+)−1] appear in (S6.11)

provenance.notes: MODE=EXTRACT for chunk C006 (S6 Turgor-limited potential growth). Included pages through Eq. S6.15 (page 38) to capture full dG0/dE derivation before S7 begins.

chunk_plan.rationale: Defines sink-limited potential growth G0 and provides analytic dG0/dE needed by the growth-optimization condition in S2.
